authored.para: This instruction converts the unsigned value in the 32-bit or 64-bit general-purpose source register to a floating-point value using the rounding mode that is specified by the FPCR, and writes the result to the SIMD&FP destination register.

This instruction can generate a floating-point exception. Depending on the settings in FPCR, the exception results in either a flag being set in FPSR or a synchronous exception being generated. For more information, see Floating-point exceptions and exception traps.
Depending on the settings in the CPACR_EL1, CPTR_EL2, and CPTR_EL3 registers, and the current Security state and Exception level, an attempt to execute the instruction might be trapped.

a.hover: Is the 16-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "32-bit to double-precision", "32-bit to half-precision", and "32-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 32, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 16-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "64-bit to double-precision", "64-bit to half-precision", and "64-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 64, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 32-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "32-bit to double-precision", "32-bit to half-precision", and "32-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 32, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 32-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "64-bit to double-precision", "64-bit to half-precision", and "64-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 64, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 64-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "32-bit to double-precision", "32-bit to half-precision", and "32-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 32, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 64-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "64-bit to double-precision", "64-bit to half-precision", and "64-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 64, encoded as 64 minus "scale".

ps.name: A64.simd_dp.float2fix.UCVTF_H32_float2fix

intro.para: Is the 16-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.

For the "32-bit to double-precision", "32-bit to half-precision", and "32-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 32, encoded as 64 minus "scale".

For the "64-bit to double-precision", "64-bit to half-precision", and "64-bit to single-precision" variants: is the number of bits after the binary point in the fixed-point source, in the range 1 to 64, encoded as 64 minus "scale".
Is the 64-bit name of the general-purpose source register, encoded in the "Rn" field.
Is the 32-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.
Is the 64-bit name of the SIMD&FP destination register, encoded in the "Rd" field.

ps.name: A64.simd_dp.float2fix.UCVTF_H32_float2fix
